data.narrative:
Northwood Space is a Series B-stage space and aerospace company founded by Bridgit Mendler and Griffin Cleverly that has raised $38M+ to date. The valuation analysis is anchored to a comparable universe of three same-sector, same-stage private companies (Aalyria, Hubble Network, and Sift) alongside five public space and aerospace peers (RKLB, PL, LUNR, ASTS, SATL).

Two valuation methods are applicable. The stage-progression method, using the $38M total raised as a proxy baseline and applying a 1.9x multiplier typical of Series B-to-next-stage advancement, implies a valuation of $72M. The peer-median method across the three private comparables yields $400M, with the peer range spanning $274M (Sift) to $1.3B (Aalyria). Public-comp anchoring via P/S multiples (sector median 59.8x across five tickers) cannot be applied due to absence of Northwood's revenue disclosure, standard for private companies.

The triangulation of two applicable methods supports a valuation range of $61M to $460M, with a median of $236M. The substantial spread reflects meaningful dispersion within the peer set and the tension between stage-based progression (conservative, $72M) and peer-median positioning (moderate, $400M). Key uncertainties include Northwood's revenue trajectory relative to peers, progress toward Series C readiness, and whether the high-valuation peers (particularly Aalyria at $1.3B) represent comparable risk and growth profiles.
